title: Fix memory_service + durable_queue bugs
analysis:
Four bugs across two service files, plus one backend file needing a new close() method.

Bug 1 (line 447, memory_service.py): `success=not (_graphiti_error and _mem0_error)` uses `and`, so it reports success=True when only ONE of the targeted stores errors. Python short-circuit: if _graphiti_error is None (Graphiti wasn't targeted or succeeded), `None and X` is `None`, `not None` is `True`. So a Mem0-only write that fails still reports success. Fix: change `and` to `or`.

Bug 2 (lines 465-467, memory_service.py): Only `_graphiti_error` is appended to the response message. If Mem0 fails, the caller sees 'Memory stored in []' with no error indication. Fix: add parallel `if _mem0_error:` block.

Bug 3 (lines 94-97, memory_service.py): `close()` only closes durable_queue and graphiti. It misses _write_journal (has close()), _event_buffer (has close()), and mem0 (needs new close()). Mem0Backend holds AsyncMemory instances with Qdrant connections that leak. Fix: add Mem0Backend.close() and call all three in MemoryService.close().

Bug 4 (lines 180-194, durable_queue.py): `enqueue_batch` starts a transaction with BEGIN but has no try/except/rollback. An exception mid-batch (e.g. a KeyError from malformed item) leaves an open transaction, potentially deadlocking subsequent operations. Fix: wrap in try/except with explicit rollback.

Test patterns: Both test files already exist with good patterns — mock_config fixture from conftest.py, MagicMock/AsyncMock for backends in test_memory_service.py, and real SQLite in tmp_path for test_durable_queue.py.

steps.description: Write failing test: add_memory success=False when single targeted store (Mem0-only) fails. In TestAddMemory, add test_success_false_when_only_targeted_store_fails: set category='preferences_and_norms' (Mem0-only, not dual_write), mock mem0.add to raise, add mock _write_journal, call add_memory, assert _write_journal.log_write_op was called with success=False. Currently passes because `not (None and error)` = `not None` = True.

Fix Bug 1: In memory_service.py line 447, change `success=not (_graphiti_error and _mem0_error)` to `success=not (_graphiti_error or _mem0_error)`. This ensures success=False when ANY store errors, not just when ALL stores error.

Write failing test: mem0 error appears in response message. In TestAddMemory, add test_mem0_error_surfaced_in_response: set category='preferences_and_norms', mock mem0.add to raise ValueError('qdrant connection refused'), call add_memory, assert 'mem0_error' substring appears in result.message. Currently fails because only _graphiti_error is appended to msg.
Fix Bug 2: After the existing `if _graphiti_error:` block (line 466-467), add parallel `if _mem0_error: msg += f' [mem0_error: {_mem0_error}]'` to surface Mem0 errors in the response message.

Write failing test: close() calls all sub-resource close methods. Add new TestClose class in test_memory_service.py. Set up service with mock _write_journal (AsyncMock with close), mock _event_buffer (AsyncMock with close), and mock mem0 (AsyncMock with close). Call service.close(). Assert all five close methods called: durable_queue.close(), graphiti.close(), mem0.close(), _write_journal.close(), _event_buffer.close(). Currently fails because close() only calls first two.

Fix Bug 3: (a) Add async def close() to Mem0Backend in mem0_client.py — iterate _instances, close each instance's vector_store.client if it has a close method, then clear _instances. (b) Update MemoryService.close() to also call self.mem0.close(), self._write_journal.close() (if set), and self._event_buffer.close() (if set). Use contextlib.suppress(Exception) around each to ensure all resources attempt cleanup.

Write failing test: enqueue_batch rolls back on mid-batch exception. Add TestEnqueueBatchRollback class in test_durable_queue.py. Enqueue a batch of 3 items where the 2nd item is missing 'operation' key (triggers KeyError). Assert the exception is raised, then check DB has 0 pending items (verifying rollback). Currently fails because no rollback leaves partial inserts.

Fix Bug 4: In durable_queue.py enqueue_batch(), replace `async with self._db.execute('BEGIN'):` with explicit transaction management: `await self._db.execute('BEGIN')` then wrap the for loop + commit in try/except, calling `await self._db.rollback()` on exception before re-raising.

design_decisions.decision: Use `or` instead of `and` for success logic
design_decisions.rationale: success=True should only be reported when ALL targeted stores succeed. With `and`, it's True when at least one store succeeds. With `or`, it's False when any store fails — correct behavior for a composite operation.

AsyncMemory from the mem0 library doesn't guarantee a close() interface. We traverse instance.vector_store.client to reach the Qdrant client's close(), using hasattr checks and contextlib.suppress to avoid crashes from library version changes.

design_decisions.decision: MemoryService.close() wraps each sub-resource close in suppress(Exception)
design_decisions.rationale: close() must be best-effort — a failure closing one resource shouldn't prevent others from being cleaned up. This matches the pattern used in GraphitiBackend.close().

design_decisions.decision: Test rollback by providing malformed batch item (missing key)
design_decisions.rationale: Using a KeyError from a missing 'operation' key is a realistic failure mode (malformed input) that tests the rollback path without needing to mock internal SQLite operations. The test verifies no items persisted by querying the DB directly.

design_decisions.decision: Journal success test uses mock _write_journal rather than real SQLite
design_decisions.rationale: The test_memory_service.py pattern uses fully mocked backends. Adding a real WriteJournal would complicate the fixture and test an integration path. Mocking lets us inspect the exact success= parameter passed to log_write_op.

reuse.how: Already used by the service fixture. No changes needed — all new tests use the existing service fixture.
Reuse for Bug 1, 2, 3 tests. For Bug 1/2, need to override mem0.add to raise. For Bug 3, need to add mock close() methods on sub-resources.
The existing fixture creates a real DurableWriteQueue in tmp_path. Can reuse for the rollback test since we need real SQLite to verify no data persisted.
Already has the close pattern (check _db, close, set None). Just need to call it from MemoryService.close().
Already has close(). Just need to call it from MemoryService.close().
